directive.title: Performance monitoring and bundle analysis setup

auditor.summary: The build implements the performance monitoring infrastructure as specified, with bundle analyzer integration, web vitals instrumentation, and a metrics ingestion endpoint. Most acceptance criteria are satisfied. However, there are two critical issues: (1) the _app.tsx modification destroys existing provider/layout structure, violating the constraint to preserve it, and (2) the vitals instrumentation has a closure bug that may cause stale reporter references. The rate limiting cleanup interval is inappropriate for serverless environments. The build is recoverable with fixes but cannot ship in current state.

top_issues.description: Existing Provider wrapping, CSS imports, and layout structure are not preserved. The generated code replaces the entire component return with just <Component {...pageProps} />, removing any existing providers, layouts, or global CSS imports that were in the original _app.tsx.

The `flushMetrics` function is called inside `createReporter`'s returned callback, but `reporter` is defined inside the same callback, creating a closure that redefines the reporter function on every metric. This means the reporter passed to `scheduleFlush` and event listeners may reference a stale closure.

The cleanup interval for rate limiting uses a module-level variable `cleanupInterval` that may not be properly cleaned up in serverless environments. In Vercel serverless functions, this interval will persist across invocations and may cause memory leaks.
